name: aligned_zero_size
reason: Alignment to size 0 is meaningless; if entailed, any address is trivially "aligned" to nothing, breaking alignment guarantees downstream.

name: inv_empty_arch
reason: A degenerate architecture with zero layers should not satisfy the invariant, as it cannot describe any valid address translation.

name: upper_vaddr_equals_base
code:
proof fn phi_3_upper_vaddr_equals_base(arch: Arch, layer: nat, base: nat)
    requires
        arch.inv(),
        arch.layers.len() > 0,
        layer < arch.layers.len(),
    ensures
        arch.upper_vaddr(layer, base) == base,
{
}
reason: If the upper virtual address equals the base for a valid architecture layer, the mapped region has zero size, meaning no addresses can be translated.

name: entry_base_non_monotone
code:
proof fn phi_4_entry_base_non_monotone(arch: Arch, layer: nat, base: nat, i: nat, j: nat)
    requires
        arch.inv(),
        layer < arch.layers.len(),
        i < j,
        j <= arch.num_entries(layer),
    ensures
        arch.entry_base(layer, base, j) <= arch.entry_base(layer, base, i),
{
}
reason: Entry base addresses must be strictly increasing with index; if a later index maps to a lower-or-equal address, entries overlap or go backwards.

name: entry_size_is_next_layer_unconstrained
code:
proof fn phi_5_entry_size_is_next_layer_unconstrained(arch: Arch)
    requires
        arch.inv(),
        arch.layers.len() == 1,
    ensures
        arch.entry_size(0) == 1,
{
}
reason: A single-layer valid architecture having entry_size of 1 byte would be far too small for any real page size, suggesting the invariant fails to lower-bound leaf entry sizes.
